title: OOF-only regime-wise Venn-Abers calibration for ensemble gating
summary: AUC itself is rank-based, so calibration only matters here as an ensemble tool.
rationale: AUC itself is rank-based, so calibration only matters here as an ensemble tool. Venn-Abers predictors are a strong public calibration method; using them only on OOF predictions can align score scales between XGB, CatBoost, ranking, and survival experts before a simple stacker decides which expert to trust by regime.
implementation_hint: Calibrate each expert on inner-fold OOF predictions, optionally separately for broad regimes such as dry/wet and early/mid/late. Feed calibrated logits or intervals into a very small meta-learner or rule-based gate rather than blending raw ranks directly.
expected_effect: May improve ensemble combination in regimes where experts have similar ordering but very different score scales, producing a small but real lift without new raw features.
risk: Calibration will not improve a single model’s AUC and can overfit if regimes are too granular or calibration is not strictly OOF.
